categories.description: The 19 core bioclimatic variables derived from monthly temperature and precipitation data (CHELSA v2.1, 1981–2010), capturing annual trends, seasonality, and extremes used in species distribution modeling.

Extended climate variables from CHELSA v2.1 (1981–2010): growing season thermal indices, snow and frost dynamics, atmospheric drivers (cloud cover, humidity, radiation, wind, vapor pressure deficit, evapotranspiration, moisture index, net primary production), and Köppen-Geiger classification.

layers.display_name: Length of Longest Growing Period above 0°C
Length of Longest Growing Period above 5°C
Length of Longest Growing Period above 10°C

categories.description: Time-window aggregate variables derived from ERA5 hourly reanalysis data, enriched at each observation's location and timestamp.

Static spatial layers describing the physical landscape: terrain (elevation and derivatives from FABDEM), soil properties (SoilGrids 2.0), landform and lithology (Hengl 2018), and land cover (GLC-FCS30D).
